evals.prompt: I just got conditionally approved for Global Entry and need to schedule my interview. Are there any open appointment slots near Chicago or Dallas in the next few months? I don't have the scanner set up yet.
evals.expected_output: Tells the user how to find location IDs for Chicago and Dallas, and either uses the CLI to check slots or guides them to install/run the scanner. Presents any available appointments clearly or explains how to monitor if nothing is open.
assertions: Mentions Chicago and Dallas locations by name or ID
Provides actionable next steps (install command or CLI command to check slots)
If no slots are found, suggests setting up monitoring
Does not hallucinate appointment times — either uses real tool or instructs user to run the CLI themselves

evals.prompt: I want to set this thing up so it runs in the background and texts me or sends a Discord message whenever a Global Entry slot opens up at JFK or Newark. What do I need to do?
evals.expected_output: Walks through installing the scanner with SMS and/or Discord extras, running setup to configure notification channels, and starting scan in the background with nohup. Includes how to stop it and watch logs.
assertions: Includes the correct pip install command (pip install global-entry-scanner)
Provides a nohup or background command to keep it running
Explains how to stop the background process
Covers Discord webhook steps OR Twilio SMS steps (or both)

evals.prompt: hey does global entry scanner work as an mcp? i have it installed already with the mcp extra. how do i hook it up to claude desktop so i can just ask you directly to check for slots?
evals.expected_output: Explains how to start the MCP server, provides the claude_desktop_config.json snippet, and explains what Claude can do once connected (check appointments, search locations, start monitoring).
assertions: Provides the exact claude_desktop_config.json snippet with correct command and args
Explains at least 2-3 things Claude can do via MCP (check slots, search locations, start scan, etc.)
Mentions that the user needs to restart Claude Desktop after editing the config
